title: Graphic Design Services

alternatives.name: Brand-identity and generalist visual-communications studios
inclusion_criteria: The establishment is classified in NAICS 541430 and is in the frozen $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Corporate-identity work plus graphic-design work not elsewhere classified is the largest of the four enumerated annual revenue families.
Typical work includes identity systems, logos, brand guidelines, packaging, signage, collateral, and related visual-communications assignments without media placement.
A tie in largest revenue family is assigned to the lexicographically smallest archetype ID.
exclusion_criteria: Web-site design plus interface and interaction design is the largest annual revenue family.
Publication design plus commercial illustration is the largest annual revenue family.
Advertising creative services, including advertising graphic design, is the largest annual revenue family.

The establishment primarily creates and places advertising campaigns, creates public-display advertising, performs programming-led web development, or sells noncommercial visual art rather than providing NAICS 541430 services.
alternatives.rationale: The 2022 Economic Census attributes 27.6% of 541430 revenue to corporate-identity design and 28.1% to graphic design not elsewhere classified. Applying their combined 55.7% revenue share to the frozen band count of 370 gives 206.1, rounded to 206. Revenue share is only a proxy for the share of firms whose primary revenue belongs to this family; the range allows for unequal firm revenue, overlapping service menus, the broad not-elsewhere-classified line, and target-band mix differences.

alternatives.name: Digital interface and web-design-led studios
inclusion_criteria: The establishment is classified in NAICS 541430 and is in the frozen $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Web-site design and development plus graphic interface and interaction design is the largest of the four enumerated annual revenue families.
The establishment remains design-led; programming-led software or systems work is not its primary activity.
A tie in largest revenue family is assigned to the lexicographically smallest archetype ID.
exclusion_criteria: Corporate identity plus graphic design not elsewhere classified is the largest annual revenue family.
Publication design plus commercial illustration is the largest annual revenue family.
Advertising creative services is the largest annual revenue family.
Custom software, systems integration, or other programming-led development is the primary activity.
alternatives.rationale: The 2022 Economic Census reports 8.2% of 541430 revenue from web-site design and development and 8.0% from graphic interface and interaction design. Applying the combined 16.2% share to 370 gives 59.9, rounded to 60. The wide range reflects overlap between the two lines, mixed-service studios, and the possibility that target-band firms differ from the all-establishment product mix.

alternatives.name: Publication-design and commercial-illustration specialists
inclusion_criteria: The establishment is classified in NAICS 541430 and is in the frozen $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Publication design plus commercial illustration is the largest of the four enumerated annual revenue families.
The commercial-art activity is performed for client communications rather than sale of noncommercial artwork.
A tie in largest revenue family is assigned to the lexicographically smallest archetype ID.
exclusion_criteria: Corporate identity plus graphic design not elsewhere classified is the largest annual revenue family.
Web-site design plus interface and interaction design is the largest annual revenue family.
Advertising creative services is the largest annual revenue family.
Independent noncommercial visual art or cartooning, printing, or publishing is the primary activity.
alternatives.rationale: The 2022 Economic Census reports 6.5% of 541430 revenue from publication design and 4.1% from commercial illustration. Applying the combined 10.6% share to 370 gives 39.2, rounded to 39. The broad range recognizes high sampling uncertainty in the smaller lines, unequal firm revenue, and frequent bundling with identity or generalist work.

alternatives.name: Advertising-creative graphic-design specialists
inclusion_criteria: The establishment is classified in NAICS 541430 and is in the frozen $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Advertising creative services, including advertising graphic design, is the largest of the four enumerated annual revenue families.
The establishment conceptualizes and produces creative artwork but does not provide the full campaign-creation and media-placement service that defines an advertising agency.
A tie in largest revenue family is assigned to the lexicographically smallest archetype ID.
exclusion_criteria: Corporate identity plus graphic design not elsewhere classified is the largest annual revenue family.
Web-site design plus interface and interaction design is the largest annual revenue family.
Publication design plus commercial illustration is the largest annual revenue family.
Creating full advertising campaigns and placing media is the primary activity.
alternatives.rationale: The 2022 Economic Census reports 7.9% of 541430 revenue from advertising creative services, including advertising graphic design. Applying 7.9% to 370 gives 29.2, rounded to 29. The range permits service-line overlap, unequal revenue per firm, and boundary error with full-service advertising agencies.

residual.name: Other, cross-over, or unclassifiable in-band graphic-design firms
residual.rationale: The four enumerated product families account for 90.4% of 2022 industry revenue and allocate 334 of the 370 frozen band firms. The residual retains the remaining 36 firms for other reported products, atypical mixes, and establishments whose largest revenue family cannot be assigned from aggregate product-line data.

sources.title: 2022 Economic Census: Industry by Products Statistics for the U.S. (ECNNAPCSIND2022)
2022 NAICS definition and cross-references for 541430 Graphic Design Services
Census Data API variables for 2022 Industry by Products Statistics
Clutch directory of graphic-design companies with service-line and employee-size fields

target_archetype.selection_basis: The frozen band has 370 firms. Aggregate 2022 Census product-line revenue shares are mapped to mutually exclusive largest-revenue-family archetypes: brand identity plus graphic design not elsewhere classified, 55.7% x 370 = 206; digital interface plus web design, 16.2% x 370 = 60; publication design plus commercial illustration, 10.6% x 370 = 39; and advertising creative, 7.9% x 370 = 29. The residual is 370 - 334 = 36. Brand-identity and generalist studios have the largest base count. Their low count of 165 remains above the next-largest high count of 100, so the supported ranges do not make the largest-base selection ambiguous. Counts are estimates, not direct firm classifications.

value_basis.scope: Cash operating costs of in-band brand-identity and generalist visual-communications studios, before owner distributions, interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization; role weights partition employee and contractor cash cost, not total revenue.

labor_contractor_cash_cost_share.rationale: No source found directly measures combined employee and contractor cash cost for the selected archetype and EBITDA band. The broad six-digit labor proxy does not isolate brand-identity/generalist studios or contractor cost. This bounded assumption allows both employee-heavy agencies and more flexible contractor-supported studios without presenting either structure as observed.
caveats: Owner labor may appear as payroll, guaranteed payment, distribution, or add-back depending on legal form and transaction reporting.
Freelance creative, photography, illustration, development, printing, and production may be recorded as cost of services, reimbursable pass-through, or operating expense.
The range is not target-specific empirical evidence and must remain an assumption.

roles.title: Graphic and brand design production
roles.rationale: Assumed share for designers producing identity systems, layouts, packaging graphics, collateral, and other visual assets.

roles.title: Creative direction and brand strategy
roles.rationale: Assumed share for creative directors, design directors, and strategists responsible for concepts, standards, review, and senior client counsel.

roles.title: Account service and project management
roles.rationale: Assumed share for scoping, scheduling, client communication, resource coordination, revisions, and delivery management.

roles.title: Digital design and production support
roles.rationale: Assumed share for digital adaptation, interaction design, presentation production, file preparation, and related technical support within a generalist studio.

roles.title: Business development, administration, and noncreative leadership
roles.rationale: Assumed share for sales, finance, people operations, office administration, and leadership time not allocated to creative strategy.

roles.title: External creative and production specialists
roles.rationale: Assumed share for freelance illustrators, photographers, copywriters, animators, developers, production artists, and other specialist contractors, excluding pure client pass-through purchases.

value_basis.rationale: The dataset supplies a six-digit labor share and a four-digit occupational mix, but neither identifies the selected service-model archetype and the occupational mix includes other specialized-design industries. A deterministic target-specific bridge is therefore unavailable. The specification freezes a transparent bounded assumption with mutually exclusive role weights summing to 1.00 instead of treating the broader mix as target evidence.
caveats: Do not substitute the broader 5414 occupational mix for these selected-archetype role weights.
Do not interpret role weights as employment shares; they are shares of employee and contractor cash cost.
Printing, media placement, materials, travel, and other reimbursable client purchases are outside the role partition.
Founder compensation classification and contractor accounting can move both the labor-contractor share and the apparent role mix.

source_hints.source: AIGA, Design Business Association, Society of Graphic Designers of Canada, agency operations benchmarks, and dated studio process documentation
source_hints.why: Use target-studio evidence on discovery, concept development, versioning, review, production, utilization, contractor use, and quality-control requirements.

source_hints.source: Agency benchmark surveys, audited design-group disclosures, transaction memoranda, customer case records, and lender quality-of-earnings materials
source_hints.why: Measure repeat-project share, retainer incidence, project duration, client concentration, procurement, rebid frequency, and switching behavior for independent branding and visual-communications studios.

source_hints.source: DealStats, BizBuySell, Axial, GF Data, design-agency brokers, and closed-transaction databases
source_hints.why: Separate current listings from closed control transactions and identify seller motivation, firm size, service mix, geography, and whether working owners remain essential.

source_hints.source: Closed lower-middle-market transactions, lender underwriting, quality-of-earnings reports, and public creative-services comparables with explicit size and business-model bridges
source_hints.why: Test entry and downside exit multiples against client concentration, project cyclicality, founder dependence, contractor use, and revenue recognition or pass-through differences.

source_hints.source: Census Business Dynamics Statistics, Business Employment Dynamics, longitudinal firm records, deal documents, and dated agency leadership histories
source_hints.why: Distinguish establishment survival from survival of a transferable studio after founder transition and test whether relationships, creative authority, and sales can persist under a new owner.

implementation: For the selected archetype, what share of cash labor and contractor cost is attached to discovery, concept creation, design production, revisions, project coordination, and final production, using evidence that distinguishes cost weights from headcount?

Which workflow steps require senior creative judgment, client workshops, or final accountability, and which can be standardized or delegated without increasing revision cycles or weakening delivery quality?
What implementation schedule, training burden, process redesign, and quality-control capacity are supported for 20-499 employee studios rather than sole practitioners or in-house design teams?
commercial_retention: What proportion of revenue is recurring retainer work, repeat projects, and one-time identity engagements, and how does that mix affect renewal, rebid, and switching behavior?
Are clients buying deliverables, access to named creative leaders, strategic judgment, production capacity, or a bundled relationship, and what evidence supports each source of retention?
How do client concentration, procurement rules, intellectual-property terms, contractor use, and pass-through production costs affect gross retention and price realization?
sale_availability: How many closed control acquisitions of independent U.S. brand-identity or generalist graphic-design studios in the target band can be verified over a defined recent window?
How many current sale listings meet the same service-model and size criteria after removing advertising agencies, printers, web-development firms, and freelance practices?
What proportion of apparent targets are founder-dependent, and what transition commitments or client-consent provisions accompany actual sales?
valuation_robustness: What entry multiples are supported by closed target-archetype transactions, and how are reported earnings normalized for owner compensation, contractor expense, pass-through revenue, and working capital?
How do downside exit multiples vary with client concentration, revenue recurrence, founder dependence, organic growth, and the proportion of project versus retainer work?
Are public-company or broader marketing-services comparables usable after explicit size, service-mix, and liquidity bridges, or must they be rejected?
operator_survival: What evidence shows that client relationships, new-business origination, creative authority, and delivery leadership survive five years after a founder or named creative principal leaves?
Which management roles must remain locally accountable, and is there a demonstrated internal successor pool in target-band studios?
How do earn-outs, founder transition periods, client concentration, key-person clauses, and portfolio attribution affect the probability that the acquired operating entity remains viable?

special_notes: This record describes brand-identity and generalist visual-communications studios, not all of NAICS 541430.

Census product lines coexist within establishments. Converting aggregate revenue shares into counts of firms classified by largest revenue family is an explicit proxy, so every count range is intentionally broad.
The frozen dataset counts firms while the Economic Census product table counts establishments; multi-establishment firms and the 2022 universe difference are additional sources of error.

The selected identity/generalist bucket combines a specific corporate-identity line with a broad not-elsewhere-classified line; target research must disclose packaging, signage, collateral, environmental, and other service mixes rather than treating the bucket as homogeneous.
The assumed value basis is target-scoped but not empirical. Preserve its role membership and weights, and do not replace it with the four-digit occupational mix.
Separate employee and specialist-contractor cash cost from printing, media, materials, photography production, travel, and other reimbursable client pass-throughs.
Treat founder labor consistently: market replacement compensation belongs in operating cash cost even when historical accounts report part of it as distributions or add-backs.
